description: One-pass review of crate secretx-aws-kms (review-rusty-crate command). Filed by /review-rusty-crate. No code changes; findings only.
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — F1 (retryable AWS errors mis-classified) is a real availability bug that contradicts the documented SecretError contract; F2 maintenance duplication will produce more drift like F1.
Correctness confidence: 75% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: F5 (DER parser laxity — I checked X.690 §10.1 from memory, not against a current spec) and F7 (KMS RSA-PSS sig size against larger keys — I read the SDK signing_algorithm spec, not run it against an RSA-3072 key). Uncertainty: I did not run integration tests (no AWS creds available) and could not observe behaviour on a live KMS endpoint with throttling enabled. Would raise confidence with: a recorded session of KmsInternalException to confirm SDK populates meta().code() vs. only the typed variant, and a local DER test suite with malformed inputs.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code is well-organised, has no unsafe, no Box<dyn Error> in public signatures, no gratuitous clones. Real idiom findings are RI-1 (missing Debug derive), RI-3 (use is_*_exception helpers), and RI-6 (hand-rolled DER parser is the structural smell that drives F5/F6). The remaining flags are clippy-level or opinion.
Rust idiom confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: RI-2 (clippy useless_format may be allowed by project policy — check workspace lint config). Uncertainty: I did not run cargo clippy with --pedantic which may surface more idiom-level lints; the standard --all-features clippy run came back clean already. Did not check the macro-expanded async_trait for hidden idiom issues; trusting that's covered by the trait owner.
title: Review: secretx-aws-kms rusty pass
